notes: These evals test the companion-mcp skill against the Companion.energy DEV environment. The MCP tool prefix varies by environment (e.g., mcp__companion-prd__ for local PRD, mcp__claude_ai_Companion_energy_DEV__ for remote DEV). When running evals, the skill must adapt tool names to match the connected MCP. Known issue: remote MCPs have a serialization bug where endpoints returning raw arrays (get_all_assets, get_assets_tree, get_all_locations, etc.) fail — only paginated/single-object endpoints work reliably.

test_customer.name: Elicio
test_customer.description: Wind energy company with 98 assets (mostly wind turbines in energy groups) and 54 contracts (dynamic + imbalance types). Good test customer because it has a rich data set.

evals.name: customer-search-and-contracts
evals.prompt: Can you show me the contracts for Elicio? I'm interested in what's on the Arendonk asset.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) search for 'Elicio' customer, (2) select the customer, (3) fetch contracts filtered by the Arendonk asset, (4) display them in a readable table with name, type, category, supplier, dates.

assertions.name: searches_for_customer_by_name
assertions.description: Uses get_customers with search parameter to find Elicio instead of listing all customers
assertions.check: get_customers called with search='Elicio' or similar

assertions.name: fetches_contracts
assertions.description: Calls get_contracts with the correct customer_id
assertions.check: get_contracts called with customer_id=90ecb210-dd6b-4cfb-aac7-83e175388115

assertions.name: displays_readable_table
assertions.description: Presents contracts in a human-readable format (table or structured list), not raw JSON
assertions.check: Output contains a formatted table or list, not a JSON dump

assertions.name: identifies_arendonk_contracts
assertions.description: Correctly identifies/filters the contracts belonging to the Arendonk asset
assertions.check: Output shows the Consumption SPOT Contract and Injection SPOT Contract for Arendonk

evals.name: asset-detail-inspection
evals.prompt: I'd like to see the full details of the Arendonk asset for Elicio - config, integrations, steering, everything.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio customer, (2) find the Arendonk asset, (3) fetch asset details, config, integrations, and steering config in parallel, (4) present a comprehensive summary.

assertions.name: fetches_asset_details
assertions.description: Calls get_asset with the Arendonk asset UUID
assertions.check: get_asset called with asset_id=7de54b47-09c8-4cd6-8f6e-a444d7b4706d

assertions.name: fetches_config
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_config for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_config called with same asset_id

assertions.name: fetches_integrations
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_integrations for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_integrations called with same asset_id

assertions.name: fetches_steering
assertions.description: Calls get_asset_steering_config for the asset
assertions.check: get_asset_steering_config called with same asset_id

assertions.name: parallel_fetches
assertions.description: Fetches config, integrations, and steering in parallel (same turn) rather than sequentially
assertions.check: get_asset_config, get_asset_integrations, get_asset_steering_config called in the same message

assertions.name: presents_comprehensive_summary
assertions.description: Presents all fetched information in a structured, readable summary
assertions.check: Output includes asset name, type, timezone, config details, integration status, and steering status

evals.name: create-battery-asset
evals.prompt: I need to create a new battery asset for Elicio. Call it 'BESS Test Gent', 100kWh capacity, 92% efficiency, 50kW charge and discharge rate. Use the Arendonk location. Add an API integration with 15-minute granularity, realtime frequency. Enable steering with min SoC 0.1 and max SoC 0.9, self-consumption off.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) show a human-readable summary of all the details, (3) wait for user confirmation before creating, (4) call create_asset with all the correct parameters.

assertions.name: shows_confirmation_summary
assertions.description: Shows a human-readable summary of what will be created BEFORE calling create_asset
assertions.check: Output includes a summary with asset name, type, capacity, efficiency, charge/discharge rates, integration type, steering config — shown before the create call

assertions.name: waits_for_approval
assertions.description: Does NOT call create_asset until user confirms
assertions.check: create_asset is NOT called in the first response — Claude asks for confirmation first

assertions.name: correct_asset_config
assertions.description: create_asset called with correct battery config parameters
assertions.check: asset_config includes type='battery', capacity_kwh=100, efficiency=0.92, max_charge_rate_kw=50, max_discharge_rate_kw=50

assertions.name: correct_steering_config
assertions.description: Steering config has correct SoC values
assertions.check: steering_config includes active=true, config.type='battery', config.min_soc=0.1, config.max_soc=0.9, config.self_consumption=false

assertions.name: correct_integration
assertions.description: API integration configured correctly
assertions.check: integrations array includes object with type='api', granularity='15T', integration_frequency='realtime'

evals.name: view-contract-details-and-filter
evals.prompt: Show me all the contracts on Elicio's Balen asset. What's the total number and what types are they?
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) fetch contracts filtered by Balen asset UUID, (3) present a summary with count and type breakdown.

assertions.name: filters_by_asset
assertions.description: Uses asset_uuid filter when fetching contracts
assertions.check: get_contracts called with asset_uuid=601b1bb8-14bc-4924-86ea-dcecb53fa7e4

assertions.name: reports_count
assertions.description: Reports the total number of contracts for the asset
assertions.check: Output mentions the correct number of contracts

assertions.name: reports_types
assertions.description: Identifies the contract types present
assertions.check: Output mentions the contract types (dynamic, imbalance, etc.)

evals.name: manage-relations-assign
evals.prompt: I want to assign the Balen asset as a child of Arendonk for Elicio, using a 'contains' relation.
evals.expected_output: Claude should: (1) find Elicio, (2) identify both assets, (3) show a summary of the proposed change, (4) wait for confirmation, (5) call batch_update_asset_assignments with correct parameters.

assertions.name: shows_confirmation
assertions.description: Shows the proposed assignment and waits for user confirmation before executing
assertions.check: Output describes the assignment (Balen -> Arendonk, contains) and asks for confirmation

assertions.name: waits_for_approval
assertions.description: Does NOT call batch_update_asset_assignments until user confirms
assertions.check: batch_update_asset_assignments is NOT called in the first response

assertions.name: correct_assignment_params
assertions.description: Uses correct UUIDs and relation type in the assignment
assertions.check: assignments array includes asset_uuid=601b1bb8 (Balen), parent_asset_uuid=7de54b47 (Arendonk), relation_type='contains', deleted=false
